summary: Test suite follows the unusual requirement of running all tests through act CI/CD, but has significant gaps. Missing fixture file definitions (junit-run1.xml, junit-run2.xml, results-run3.json, empty-suite.xml referenced in tests but not provided) makes the suite non-reproducible. No unit tests for XML/JSON parsing functions, normalizeStatus(), error handling, or edge cases (malformed files, invalid statuses, missing fields). Coverage is limited to end-to-end happy path and one alternative scenario (pass-only). Workflow structure tests are reasonable. Act integration tests use exact output string matching, which is brittle—should parse markdown output instead. Lazy caching strategy avoids redundant act runs (good for perf) but unconventional test design. Tests would catch output format regressions but miss implementation bugs in parsing, error paths, and boundary conditions. If fixture files exist as intended, coverage improves to 3/5; without them, tests cannot run.
